slides.title: 'Healthy' foods that *crash your energy*.
slides.sub: Granola, smoothies, and flavored yogurt can hit like dessert — then drop you an hour later.

slides.title: Health-halo labels
slides.body: 'Natural' and 'low-fat' often means *added sugar* to fix the taste. The label hides it well.

slides.title: Check these first
slides.body: Granola, flavored yogurt, smoothies, and 'protein' bars can pack *20–40g of sugar* a serving.

slides.title: Spike then drop
slides.body: A fast sugar rush spikes blood sugar, then the dip *leaves you tired* and hungry an hour later.

slides.title: Find added sugars
slides.body: Flip the label and read *'added sugars'* — under 6g a serving is a solid rule of thumb.

slides.title: Build a steadier plate
slides.body: Pair carbs with *protein or fiber* to blunt the spike — plain yogurt and berries beats the sweetened cup.

slides.title: Spot hidden sugar *in a snap*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health breaks down the sugar for you, so the sneaky stuff stops surprising you.

caption:
Your 'healthy' breakfast might be hitting like dessert. 🍩

Granola, smoothies and flavored yogurt can carry 20–40g of sugar — that's the spike-then-crash. Read 'added sugars' and pair carbs with protein. Save this. 🔖

Snap a meal and Vibe Health breaks down the hidden sugar for you → link in bio.
youtube_title: The 'Healthy' Foods Quietly Crashing Your Energy
